name: Garbea and Kolitza Loop from Balmaseda
name_original: Garbea y Kolitza desde Balmaseda — circular por la Zona Especial de Conservación de Ordunte
description: Hike the challenging Garbea and Kolitza circular route from Balmaseda, traversing the Ordunte Special Area of Conservation for panoramic vie
region_intro: Hiking around Balmaseda offers diverse and scenic landscapes in southwestern Biscay. The region is characterized by the Ordunte mountains, featuring prominent peaks like Kolitza and Garbea, which provide extensive panoramic views. River valleys, including the Kadagua and its tributaries, create lush riparian environments. Trails often traverse forests and fields, showcasing varied natural scenery. Best hiking trails around Balmaseda The most popular hiking route is Garbea and Kolitza Loop from Balmaseda, an 11.6 miles (18.7 km) trail that takes 6 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views from the summits of Garbea and Kolitza. Another top favourite among local hikers is Kolitza Parking – Refugio Kolitza loop from Balmaseda, a moderate 7.9 miles (12.8 km) path. This trail ascends towards Mount Kolitza, providing views of the surrounding valleys. Local hikers also love the Kolitza Parking – Refugio Kolitza loop from Ermita de San Isidro, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 57 minutes. Hiking around Balmaseda is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and diverse forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate ascents to challenging long-distance paths. The routes in Balmaseda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Balmaseda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Balmaseda?
A: Balmaseda offers a diverse network of over 30 hiking trails. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including 11 easy, 17 moderate, and 6 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: What are the typical terrain and surface conditions of hiking trails in Balmaseda?
A: The trails around Balmaseda primarily feature mountainous terrain, often winding through forests and fields. You can expect varied surfaces, from well-trodden paths to more rugged sections, especially on ascents to peaks like Kolitza and Garbea. Some routes also follow riverbanks, offering shaded walks through riparian environments.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Balmaseda?
A: Yes, Balmaseda offers several easy and moderate trails that are suitable for families. These routes often provide a chance to explore the natural beauty without significant difficulty, sometimes passing through historic areas or recreational zones like Bolumburu. Look for trails categorized as 'easy' or 'moderate' for suitable options.

Q: What kind of panoramic views can I expect on hikes around Balmaseda?
A: Hiking in Balmaseda rewards you with spectacular panoramic views, especially from the summits of the Ordunte mountains like Kolitza, Garbea, and Pelayos. From these vantage points, you can often see the Encartaciones, Valle de Mena, Valle de Aiala, Salcedo Valley, and even the distant sea on clear days. A popular route for such views is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058212990/garbea-and-kolitza-loop-from-balmaseda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbea and Kolitza Loop from Balmaseda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the hiking trails?
A: Yes, many trails in Balmaseda integrate the town's rich historical heritage with its natural beauty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Roque Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, a religious site dating back to the 13th century, is located at the summit of Mount Kolitza, making the climb both a physical and cultural experience. Some local routes also pass through historic areas like Santo Domingo and Cerro del Castillo.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Balmaseda?
A: Yes, Balmaseda features several excellent circular hiking routes. A notable example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058212990/garbea-and-kolitza-loop-from-balmaseda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbea and Kolitza Loop from Balmaseda&lt;/a&gt;, which covers both Garbea and Kolitza peaks. Another popular option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5343037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kolitza Parking – Refugio Kolitza loop from Balmaseda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Balmaseda?
A: Balmaseda's diverse landscapes offer varied natural scenery throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, making them ideal for hiking. Summer can be warm, but routes along riverbanks like the Ayega offer cool, shaded walks. Winter hikes are possible, especially on lower elevation trails, but higher mountain routes may require more preparation due to weather conditions.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Balmaseda?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near popular trailheads. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5343037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kolitza Parking – Refugio Kolitza loop from Balmaseda&lt;/a&gt; indicate designated parking areas, providing convenient access for hikers.

Q: What are some challenging hikes in the Balmaseda area?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Balmaseda offers several demanding routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058212990/garbea-and-kolitza-loop-from-balmaseda" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garbea and Kolitza Loop from Balmaseda&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.7 km trail with significant elevation gain. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38172002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Ordunte Reservoir – Burgüeno Summit loop from Balmaseda&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 24 km and involves substantial climbing.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Balmaseda?
A: The hiking routes in Balmaseda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 90 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse and scenic landscapes, the rewarding panoramic views from mountain summits, and the variety of trails suitable for different abilities. The integration of natural beauty with historical landmarks is also a highlight for many.

Q: Are there any natural parks or caves nearby worth exploring?
A: While not directly within Balmaseda, the region offers access to significant natural attractions. The Nature Park of Valley of Karrantza, home to the notable Pozalagua Cave, is easily accessible. Additionally, the Armañón Natural Park, which contains the impressive Torca del Carlista Cavern, is another significant natural attraction in the vicinity that adds to the regional appeal for nature enthusiasts.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay near the hiking trails in Balmaseda?
A: Balmaseda, as a historic town, offers various amenities for visitors. You'll find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options within the town itself, many of which are conveniently located near starting points for local trails, such as San Severino Square. These establishments provide excellent opportunities to refuel and relax after a day of hiking.
tips: The best-known image of Balmaseda, and it is right. A pleasure to go through.

Religious sanctuary from the 15th century, located in the Plaza Mayor of Balmaseda. The building is Gothic in style, but due to some reforms carried out in the 18th century, some Baroque additions can be seen.

The hermitage of San Roque is located on a hill, at the top of Mount Kolitza, on the edge of a precipice trying to maintain balance. At 880 meters of altitude. Although it is already documented in 1111, the chapel that remains standing is the XIII, in which the Cistercian style prevailed.

Built in ashlar masonry, it has a single rectangular nave, with a barrel vault and apse. The main entrance is under a semicircular arch, with three archivolts resting on corbels with figures. The tower has a single bell and a stone cross on the top.
Summit located in the Ordunte Special Conservation Zone. It is easily accessible as it can be reached by a track that reaches the communications antenna at the top.

faqs.question: Where does the Garbea and Kolitza circular hike start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: This circular route begins and ends in Balmaseda. You can find parking within the town itself, which serves as the primary access point for the trail.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is generally considered difficult. It requires a good to very good fitness level due to significant altitude gain (over 860 meters) and constant ascents and descents. Sure-footedness is also essential for some sections.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Garbea and Kolitza circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied, taking you through a mix of unpaved tracks, paved tracks, gravel paths, natural paths, and even some streets within Balmaseda. You'll traverse forests, fields, and open areas, with abundant vegetation providing shade on hot days.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable sights. In Balmaseda, you'll pass the Old Bridge of Balmaseda and the Church of San Severino. As you ascend Mount Kolitza, you'll find the San Roque Hermitage on Mount Kolitza, a rare 13th-century Romanesque construction, and a historic 'Nevera' (ice house). The Garbea Peak (718 m), known as the "Balcón de Las Encartaciones," offers spectacular panoramic views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed for much of the year. However, the abundant vegetation provides welcome shade on hot days, suggesting spring and early autumn are ideal for comfortable temperatures. The Kolitza Balmatrail, a mountain sports event, is held around October, indicating good conditions then.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in the Special Area of Conservation of Ordunte?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or access the Special Area of Conservation of Ordunte. However, always respect local regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: Is the Garbea and Kolitza circular hike dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, it's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through livestock areas or within the Special Area of Conservation of Ordunte, to protect wildlife and ensure their safety.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with other official trails. You will find it intersects with parts of the Bizkaiko bira, Camino Olvidado (Bilbao-Villasante), Puente Viejo o de la muza, and Bidegorri.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the peaks?
faqs.answer: From the summit of Mount Kolitza, you'll get expansive views of the surrounding landscape. From La Garbea, often called the "Balcón de Las Encartaciones," you can enjoy panoramic vistas stretching across the Salcedo Valley, the peaks of Ubieta, Larrea, Montes de Triano, Mello, Montes de Saldamando, Muskiz, and even the sea on clear days.

faqs.question: Is there a mountain refuge or facilities near the summit of Kolitza?
faqs.answer: Yes, near the San Roque Hermitage on Mount Kolitza, about 100 meters away, there is a mountain refuge. This can be a useful spot for a break or shelter.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of Mount Kolitza?
faqs.answer: Mount Kolitza is one of the "montes bocineros" (horn-blowing mountains) of Bizkaia. Historically, horns were blown from its summit, along with Oiz, Gorbea, Sollube, and Ganekogorta, to call general assemblies.

waypoints.name: San Roque Hermitage on Mount Kolitza
